title: Woodland sachet
subtitle: A dry botanical sachet with cedarwood, vetiver, and lavender for drawers and wardrobes
excerpt: A woodland sachet combines dried botanicals, wood chips, and essential oils in a cotton muslin bag. Cedarwood chips repel moths; vetiver root acts as a fixative that anchors the lighter scent notes and extends the life of the sachet. Place in drawers, wardrobes, or near shoes. Refresh the scent by adding a few more drops of essential oil when it fades.
sourceNotes: Dry botanical sachet. Cedarwood chips (red cedar, Juniperus virginiana): natural moth deterrent; distinctive woody scent. Vetiver root (Vetiveria zizanioides): fixative; slows evaporation of lighter scent compounds from the blend. Dried lavender buds: familiar sachet scent, also moth deterrent. Dried rosemary: supporting herbal note. Essential oils added to dry materials for longer-lasting scent. Muslin bags: 10 x 15 cm or similar.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally for more sachets. Rest the loose mix for 24 hours before filling bags for the scent to distribute evenly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix the botanicals and oils 24 hours in advance for a more even scent distribution.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months before the scent noticeably fades. Refresh by adding 2 to 3 drops of cedarwood or lavender essential oil to the inside of the bag. Cedarwood chips remain effective as a moth deterrent even after the scent has faded.

glossaryTerms.definition: An ingredient that slows the evaporation of lighter fragrance compounds in a blend, extending the overall scent life. Vetiver root and oakmoss are classic natural fixatives for dry sachets and potpourri.

Cedar, lavender, and rosemary are natural ingredients that moths find unpleasant. A sachet containing these materials in a wardrobe makes the environment less attractive to moths. They do not kill moths or larvae; they deter new moths from settling.

content.text: here: it anchors the lighter lavender and cedarwood notes and extends the sachet's useful life significantly. Cedarwood chips and lavender both work as
in wardrobes. Allow the loose mix to rest for 24 hours before filling the muslin bags; the essential oils distribute more evenly through the dry material when given time.

items.prepNote: Red cedar (Juniperus virginiana); primary scent and moth deterrent
Dried; supporting scent and moth deterrent
Add to dry mix; boosts cedarwood chip scent

content.text: Combine all dry botanicals in the mixing bowl. Drizzle the essential oil(s) over the dry material and stir to distribute. Cover and leave for 24 hours so the oils absorb into the botanical matter.
Divide the mix between the two muslin bags. Tie the drawstrings tightly.
Place in a drawer, on a wardrobe shelf, or in a shoe rack. When the scent fades, open the bag and add 2 to 3 drops of cedarwood or lavender essential oil, reseal, and the sachet is active again.
12 months before the initial scent fades. Refreshable with essential oil drops as above. Cedarwood chips remain effective as a moth deterrent even when the added scent has faded.
